mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 235 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to elevated real interest rates that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 235 exceed 1.5% on average, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns falls below -0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signal a tightening labor market that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the combined effect reverses direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals expanding industrial capacity utilization, which correlates with stronger corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO changes (p > 0.10) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in HOUST and RSAFS indicates strong housing and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market that pressures firms to raise wages, creating inflationary pressures that lead to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 1% in 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5, or the directional correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal aggressive monetary tightening amid inflation, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL explains less than 5% of SPX return variance, or the combined effect reverses direction in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2% (GDPC1) indicates robust economic expansion, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 changes (p > 0.10) in 3-year rolling windows.
